name: inflight_vmem_region_size_zero_on_failed_unmap
reason: A failed unmap (Err result) produces a zero-size inflight region — this means failed unmaps never block other operations via overlap, even though they're still in a non-Idle state occupying the core

proof fn phi_2_pte_size_unmap_waiting_depends_on_pt(vaddr: nat, ult_id: nat, pt1: Map<nat, PTE>, pt2: Map<nat, PTE>, pte: PTE)
    requires
        pt1.contains_key(vaddr),
        pt1[vaddr] == pte,
        !pt2.contains_key(vaddr),
    ensures
        os::CoreState::UnmapWaiting { ult_id, vaddr }.pte_size(pt1) == pte.frame.size,
        os::CoreState::UnmapWaiting { ult_id, vaddr }.pte_size(pt2) == 0,
{
}
reason: `UnmapWaiting` and early `UnmapExecuting` compute pte_size from the current pt map — if pt changes between when the unmap started and when size is queried, the inflight region size changes, potentially allowing overlapping operations to slip through

proof fn phi_5_candidate_overlaps_inflight_zero_size_unmap_err(pt: Map<nat, PTE>, vaddr: nat, base: nat, size: nat)

        base != vaddr,
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !os::candidate_mapping_overlaps_inflight_vmem(
            pt,
            set![os::CoreState::UnmapOpDone { ult_id: 0, vaddr, result: Err(()) }],
            base,
            size,
        ),

}
reason: A failed UnmapOpDone has zero-size inflight region — it never blocks any candidate mapping at a different base, effectively making the core invisible to overlap checks while still being non-Idle and occupying the core state
